etymology.description: 旡 (phonetic) + 心 (heart) -> 㤅; 夊 (foot) added -> 𢙴; top 旡 corrupted to ⿱爫冖 -> 愛
etymology.interpretation: The original form 㤅 combined the phonetic element 旡 with the semantic component 心 (heart), symbolizing the emotional nature of love. To depict the involvement of people, the redundant component 夊 (foot) was later added, changing the form to 𢙴. Over time, the top phonetic component 旡 was transformed into ⿱爫冖, resulting in the current character 愛, which continues to convey the concept of love from the heart, with nuances of involving or interacting with others.
etymology.simplification: The lower component of 愛, 夊, was replaced with 友 (yǒu, "friend") in the simplified form 爱 to reduce strokes while retaining a conceptually related element.

examples.chinese: 她的微笑让我感到温暖和爱(她的微笑讓我感到溫暖和愛)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de wēixiào ràng wǒ gǎndào wēnnuǎn hé ài.
examples.english: Her smile fills me with warmth and love.

examples.chinese: 父母总是无条件地爱我们(父母總是無條件地愛我們)。
examples.pinyin: Fùmǔ zǒng shì wú tiáojiàn dì ài wǒmen.

examples.chinese: 爱是人生中最重要的情感(愛是人生中最重要的情感)。
examples.pinyin: Ài shì rénshēng zhōng zuì zhòngyào de qínggǎn.
examples.english: Love is the most important emotion in life.
